report_name: CISA Discovers 'FIRESTARTER' Backdoor on Federal Cisco Firewall; Malware Survives Patches

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: An APT actor exploited two vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362) in a public-facing Cisco Firepower device to gain initial access and deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor.

attack_patterns.name: Unix Shell
attack_patterns.description: The LINE VIPER post-exploitation toolkit was used to execute arbitrary command-line interface (CLI) commands on the compromised Cisco device.

attack_patterns.name: Event Triggered Execution
attack_patterns.description: The FIRESTARTER backdoor achieved persistence by installing a hook within the Cisco device's core LINA processing engine, ensuring its code was executed upon specific system events.

attack_patterns.name: Hijack Execution Flow
attack_patterns.description: FIRESTARTER evaded defenses by hooking into LINA, a core, trusted system process on the Cisco firewall, thereby hijacking its execution flow to run malicious code.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: The LINE VIPER toolkit was used to harvest user credentials from the compromised Cisco firewall device.

attack_patterns.name: Network Sniffing
attack_patterns.description: The LINE VIPER toolkit conducted packet captures to monitor network traffic passing through the compromised firewall.

malware.name: FIRESTARTER
malware.description: A sophisticated backdoor found on a Cisco Firepower device that provides persistent remote access by hooking into the device's core LINA processing engine, allowing it to survive firmware updates and patches.

malware.name: LINE VIPER
malware.description: A post-exploitation toolkit deployed via the FIRESTARTER backdoor. It enables attackers to execute CLI commands, capture network packets, bypass VPN authentication, and harvest credentials from the compromised Cisco device.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-20333
vulnerabilities.description: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in Cisco ASA and FTD software exploited by an APT actor to gain initial access and deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: The KEV due date refers to the deadline by which FCEB agencies are expected to review and begin implementing the guidance outlined in Emergency Directive (ED) 25-03 (URL listed below in Notes). Agencies must follow the mitigation steps provided by CISA (URL listed below in Notes) and vendor’s instructions (URL listed below in Notes). Adhere to the applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are not available.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Cisco Secure Firewall Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) and Secure Firewall Threat Defense (FTD) Buffer Overflow Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-20362
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Cisco ASA and FTD software exploited alongside CVE-2025-20333 to gain initial access to a federal agency's firewall.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: The KEV due date refers to the deadline by which FCEB agencies are expected to review and begin implementing the guidance outlined in Emergency Directive (ED) 25-03 (URL listed below in Notes). Agencies must follow the mitigation steps provided by CISA (URL listed below in Notes) and vendor’s instructions (URL listed below in Notes). Adhere to the applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are not available.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Cisco Secure Firewall Adaptive Security (ASA) Appliance and Secure Firewall Threat Defense (FTD) Missing Authorization Vulnerability

identities.name: U.S. Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) Agency
identities.description: An unnamed U.S. federal agency whose Cisco Firepower device was compromised with the FIRESTARTER backdoor.

indicators.name: Cisco ASA/FTD Packet Capture Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Cisco ASA/FTD Packet Capture Detection
id: 2b1f8c9a-0d3e-4f1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects packet capture commands being executed on Cisco ASA/FTD devices, a capability of the LINE VIPER toolkit used in conjunction with the FIRESTARTER backdoor.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/firestarter-backdoor-hit-federal-cisco.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - attack.t1040
  - attack.collection
level: high
logsource:
  category: firewall
  product: cisco
  service: asa
detection:
  selection:
    keywords:
      - 'capture '
      - 'packet-capture'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects commands related to packet captures on Cisco network devices, which is a known capability of the LINE VIPER toolkit.

relationships.source_name: U.S. Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) Agency
